title: Lab 50: Systemd Timer Unit
description: Create and enable a persistent systemd timer for RHCSA 10 practice

tasks: Create /usr/local/sbin/rhcsa-timer10.sh so it appends the text TIMER10 OK to /var/log/rhcsa-timer10.log.
Create a systemd oneshot service named rhcsa-timer10.service that runs /usr/local/sbin/rhcsa-timer10.sh.
Create rhcsa-timer10.timer so it runs the service every 5 minutes, is persistent, and starts automatically at boot.
hints: Use Type=oneshot for the service unit.
Use OnCalendar=*:0/5 and Persistent=true in the timer unit.
Enable and start the timer, not the service.
checks: systemctl cat rhcsa-timer10.service | grep -Fqx 'ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/rhcsa-timer10.sh' && systemctl cat rhcsa-timer10.service | grep -Fqx 'Type=oneshot'
systemctl cat rhcsa-timer10.timer | grep -Fqx 'OnCalendar=*:0/5' && systemctl cat rhcsa-timer10.timer | grep -Fqx 'Persistent=true' && systemctl is-enabled rhcsa-timer10.timer | grep -qx enabled
solution_outline: Create the script, create matching service and timer units, reload systemd, then enable the timer.
